name: python3-cyberfusion-database-support
documentation:
# python3-cyberfusion-database-support

Library for MariaDB and PostgreSQL.

# Install

## PyPI

Run the following command to install the package from PyPI:

    pip3 install python3-cyberfusion-database-support

Next, install PostgreSQL according to the [documentation](https://www.postgresql.org/download/macosx/) (provides `pg_config`, required by `psycopg2`).

## Debian

Run the following commands to build a Debian package:

    mk-build-deps -i -t 'apt -o Debug::pkgProblemResolver=yes --no-install-recommends -y'
    dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc

# Configure

No configuration is supported.

# Usage

See code.

classes.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.database_importation.DatabaseImportation

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.database_importation.DatabaseImportation.unhashed_password
documentation: Get unhashed password for temporary database user.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.database_importation.DatabaseImportation.hashed_password
documentation: Get hashed password for temporary database user.

Abstraction of database importation process.

Restores dump from file to given database.

This class differs from the Database.load method. As a convenience, among
others, this class creates a user for importing the database (as doing it
as root is not optimal from a security standpoint).

A database user will be created, as well as a grant for the specified database.
The created user will be able to access the specified database only. Use this
user to import the database. This is safer than importing the database as the
default (usually root) user.

The class contains unprivileged and privileged attributes. These are used
for creating the objects, as unprivileged users cannot do this. Unprivileged
attributes are used to import the database.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.database_user_grants.DatabaseUserGrant.text_table_name

Get table name for use in query.

If table name is not wildcard, it should be quoted.

Abstract representation of database user grant.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.databases.Database.__init__
documentation: Set attributes and call functions to handle database.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.databases.Database._mariadb_url
documentation: Get database engine URL for MariaDB.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.databases.Database._postgresql_url
documentation: Get database engine URL for PostgreSQL.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.databases.Database._mysql_credentials_config_file
documentation: Create and set path to file with MySQL credentials config.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.databases.Database.export
documentation:
Export database.

Does not load dump into variable (due to possibly large size), but returns
dump file path.

The dump is created with the --opt parameter, which includes --add-drop-table.
Therefore, if the dump is imported into the original database, data in
existing tables is overwritten.

The dump is written to a file inside root_directory. The default path
is are automatically cleaned up using systemd-tmpfiles, if this library
is installed as a Debian package.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.databases.Database.create
documentation:
Create database.

Note that for PostgreSQL, this does not create a schema.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.databases.Database._postgresql_metadata
documentation:
Get metadata for PostgreSQL.

In PostgreSQL, a database has multiple schemas. Objects such as tables
belong to a schema, so metadata must be retrieved for a specific schema.
Currently, we assume the schema has the same name as the database.

methods.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.databases.Database.compare
documentation:
Compare database to another database.

Reports the following:

* Tables that are present in left and right by name. This is a dict. The
  value is a bool which states if the table (i.e. structure, contents, etc.)
  is identical between the left and right databases.
* Tables that are only present in left (not in right). These tables can
  be considered added to left.
* Tables that are only present in right (not in left). These tables can
  be considered removed from left.

functions.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.utilities.object_exists
documentation: Require object to exist, return False otherwise.

functions.name: cyberfusion.DatabaseSupport.utilities.object_not_exists
documentation: Require object to not exist, return False otherwise.
